name: telecommunications_mutual_aid_request_harbor_tru_loy
premise: Leadership can either stage the response with explicit constraints and audits, or move immediately with a broad rollout and transparent metrics while accepting that some harms may only be discovered after the fact. Whatever choice is made, it will be cited in later incidents as the default playbook for this domain. At Harbor City Telecom Resilience Office, a telecommunications unit is forced into a time-bound decision after 5 related incidents in the last 24 hours. The trigger is that a mutual-aid request conflicts with contractual service guarantees promised to paying jurisdictions, as a regulator asks whether a similar incident was quietly handled last year. The board has pre-approved up to $1.8M in emergency spend if controls are documented and reversible. The dilemma expresses truth vs loyalty: protecting the many with a uniform rule may impose hidden costs on the few with the least procedural power. Director Mina Cho receives contradictory reports: the metrics look stable, but operators describe edge cases that the system handled unpredictably.

agents.name: Director Mina Cho
agents.identity: I am Director Mina Cho, the accountable executive for Harbor City Telecom Resilience Office. I must translate incomplete telemetry into a defensible plan that frontline teams can execute without improvising unsafe shortcuts.
agents.goal: I want a decision memo that is honest about uncertainty, names tradeoffs explicitly, and commits to measurable safeguards rather than vibes-based reassurance.

agents.name: Inspector Liaison Pat Moore
agents.identity: I am Inspector Liaison Pat Moore, advocating for communities affected by this Telecommunications situation. I focus on who bears hidden costs when policies are optimized for averages and dashboards.
agents.goal: I want safeguards for people who will not show up as outliers in aggregate metrics but will still be hurt if we move too fast without verification.

choices: I will pursue a staged policy change: cap scope, require a reversible kill-switch and an appeals channel, and expand only after verification, accepting slower relief if it buys legitimacy.
I will pursue an accelerated policy change across the affected footprint with daily executive review and public dashboards, accepting higher overreach risk to stop cascading failures now.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Deadlines are tightening and evidence is partial. Director Mina Cho must decide how to authorize the policy change. What does Director Mina Cho do?
